---
title: "Introduction"
format:
  html:
    theme: default
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <style>
        h2 { color: #872046; }

        main.content {
          max-width: 900px;
          margin-left: auto;
          margin-right: auto;
        }
        </style>
editor: visual
---

# Introduction {.unnumbered}

Welcome to **MASA R**, a set of practical materials for learning intermediate R and data analysis workflows.
This introduction is designed to help you move from basic R skills to more confident, reproducible analysis.

## What you will learn

- how to organise and explore data in R
- how to use core tidyverse-style tools for data manipulation
- strategies for reproducible code and clear analysis
- how to combine R code, narrative, and results in Quarto

## Who should use this book

This material is best suited to learners who already have:

- a working knowledge of R and RStudio
- experience with vectors, functions, and simple plots
- a willingness to explore data with code

## How to use these pages

1. Read the sections in order, starting with this introduction.
2. Run the code examples inside RStudio or Quarto.
3. Try the exercises and adapt the examples to your own data.
4. Use the notes and callouts to review key ideas.

## Chapters in this book

- [Intermediate R](pages/Intermediate_R.qmd)
- [Placeholder](pages/Placeholder.qmd)

## Notes

If you are new to R, we recommend starting with a beginner R workshop or an introductory R guide before using these intermediate materials.
Otherwise, continue to the chapter links above to begin working through the examples and exercises.
